author: Deeksha A.
pros: The user interface and ease of use. We can also switch between table and Kanban view.
cons: There is nothing to dislike. It solves the purpose like a charm.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Incredibly effective platform for communicating with clients on web development
description: Love it. Small hiccups here and there but excellent product and does what I need it to do.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: *Ease of use: clients who self-identify as "not a computer whiz" had no trouble using the platform and features. *Ability for client to view a webpage, click somewhere on it, make a comment about that area, and move on...all while tasks get created and organized in a "to do" list gets generated in the background...makes client feedback a BREEZE. *Effective tracking: even if the client isn't labeling comments, tasks, requests, etc "properly", it's very easy to follow activity and make sure nothing gets lost or forgotten *Pricing is appropriate and worth it *It does everything I needed for a remote website development project: get client feedback, revise, and complete with no crossed wires or forgotten/lost requests. And no misunderstandings or miscommunications - the record is right there! Total project management solution.
cons: When the "collaborator" (ability to add a "client" is an enterprise only, custom price point) provides feedback, the task status isn't automatically changed from "waiting on client feedback" - so the "collaborator" (ie, my client) has to manually change the status to something else - otherwise the task, although provided with feedback, remains in the "I'm waiting on you" stage. The method for changing the status is simple - but clients don't need to be bothered with the multi-step process of making sure to adjust "tags". Having said that, the way to go about making sure that the client has in fact provided feedback (despite the unchanged "status") is to simply sort tasks by activity. Which is where we arrive at the main issue I have: At the time of this writing, you could sort tasks by date that a task was modified - but you can't add a secondary filter (ie: "modified BY"). Which means that while a client may have provided feedback and forgotten to change the status, I'm simultaneously modifying or adjusting other tasks.... hence I'm not quickly and easily able to filter the list for tasks that the client has recently modified. It's not the end of the world - it just created a bit of extra work having to go through the "Recently modified" tasks one-by-one to make sure that I hadn't missed anything. Yes, I can go through the list of "waiting on client" ....but completed tasks, where client changed their mind & added a revision w/o changing status away from "completed".....

review_title: Great platform, would love to see some of the functionality expanded.
description: I previously used Airtable for tracking web change requests, and I loved the functionality of this platform however it could be hard for clients to utilise without seeing the comments in the context of the website. Some suggestions: - Allow drag & drop sorting of tasks - Allow task list to remember your sort order from last time instead of defaulting to post date, eg. I don't have to select to sort by 'Screen' each time I reload the page. - Allow screenshot option that captures the entire page - eg. https://chrome.google.com/webstore/detail/full-page-screen-capture/fdpohaocaechififmbbbbbknoalclacl That way, I could add all of my feedback comments within one screen, and when sorting or filtering tasks we can focus on feedback for one particular page. - If you could rename screens within the individual task window, that would be even better. - If I create a task without taking a screenshot, I lose the ability to name the Screen according to it's location. - Can you enable the Screen field within all tasks so they can be named & sorted regardless of having an attached screenshot? Or, allow us to add a screenshot after the task has been created, and then I can rename it. - Include a page link within each task so you click straight through to the website (for example, on hover of the screenshot image, include an icon that links to the website page) - Allow formatting of text within the task description (eg. bold, underline, bullets) - Allow 'Select All' or option on task filters
author: Justine W.
pros: Screenshot functionality with comments shown on page, ability to post comments within a stream although these can be difficult to read. If status changes were a different colour that would help.
cons: Can be a clunky user experience, especially from a client perspective, so it can be hard to collaborate within this platform. They tend to resort to emailing their feedback instead of creating tasks. Do you have any basic user guides for logging in, accessing the website review page, creating tasks and tagging users or reassigning?
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

author: Verified Reviewer
pros: -Simple and easy way to track needed edits -Ability to see exact locations of needed changes -Collaboration of the entire team and client in one place -Tracking all changes throughout the life of a project -Customer support
cons: -Missing a few features, like the ability to archive older versions / feedback, but these are being rolled out continuously -Occasional down times, but this is getting less frequent -User interface is still a little clunky and needs some updates

review_title: The easiest & most useful, but not the only place to find jobs
description: i wish more job boards made it this easy to apply. i also like the feature that lets me know if a company is interested in me. i guess another thing would be that i've yet to get a job through this site.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: lots of local listings, very easy to use and apply for jobs
cons: hard to find remote jobs, not all job matches are a true match, too many emails matching me to jobs that don't match, and misleading headlines in the emails saying there are certain types of jobs available, but when i look at the listing it doesn't add up

description: We are a mid-tier manufacturer who has to organize jobs by contract, shipping destination, and timeline. zipBoard allows us to see all of our jobs in one place and easily organize them by priority, especially in this busy time of the year.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: The user interface is very easy to navigate and nothing to tucked behind confusing menu options. When you click on an option, you get what you click and nothing else.
cons: The color scheme can sometimes be a bit glaring if you're working late at night with your laptop as the only light source. Not a huge problem, but something worth noting.

description: Client review and feedback is made much easier and faster with zB.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: It makes working with clients for reviews seamless and saves SO much time. Efficiency is a huge reason why we went with zipBoard.
cons: Nothing of note. We have had a very wonderful experience so far.

review_title: Excellent tool to review video content
description: Education, E-Learning, Media Industry needs this tool. Expecting a world class review product near future. It has all features to be at the top. Fluid and design flow of the website should be enhanced
author: Pavithra S.
pros: 1) Clients can view without login 2) Clients can comment on the timeline where changes are to be done 3) Ready for any kind of collaboration with freelancers from different teams and clients at different locations
cons: 1) Design and flow can be world class to make this product shine 2) Loading and Lag issues 3) Names should be customizable
author_duration: Used the software for: I used a free trial

review_title: Perfect tool for processing own website relaunch
author: Lucas H.
pros: zipBoard was the perfect tool in the process of our website relaunch to show our programmers what bugs are still there or what is missing to finish the website. It was easy to use and it took only 10 minutes to familiarize yourself with the tool.
cons: The only thing we didn't quite like was the fact that you could only take screenshots of your website and could only make your markers in the screenshots.
author_duration: Used the software for: I used a free trial

review_title: A good product to take design specifications
description: As a developer it was easy to understand the design problems, and what to fix, when posting a screenshot, and assign a tasks for the changes on it. It is really helpful, when the reviewer is far away.
author: Gábor M.
pros: Love the feature to comment on items, create tasks about design changes.
cons: Because I am new to the product, it was hard to find some function.
author_duration: Used the software for: I used a free trial

review_title: Great tool to review eLearning projects
description: zipBoard definitely sped up our workflow and now we have less errors thanks to the software. There is still room for improvement and I like that the team is constantly working on new features and is very supportive when we want to add a feature or change something.
author: Michaela P.
pros: I like the ease of use of zipBoard and that I can take screenshots and point the person in charge to the exact location. The filter options are also great.
cons: The software is sometimes buggy and freezes. When we work on a large project, it sometimes does not work. We've had clients refusing to use the software because of firewall restrictions.

review_title: Its a progressive approach to QA tracking, can still better UX functionality
description: I experience zipboard as a developer, so my tasks are uploading files, addressing QA edits and changing status of tasks so QA can sign off bugs, etc. Its been a good experience and I've heard that customer service is super attentive but the tool needs to refine their overall user experience for shortcuts, etc. Its a really great concept and has potential to more success. I think the branding identity is weak and does not portray much credibility but maybe that can be re-designed later on.
author: Patrick V.
pros: The interface concept is simple and easy to use, even though it still has some sections where it could be better. There are big buttons for you to click on tasks, upload files, add collaborators and share your projects.
cons: For developers reviewing client or internal issues, you have to go back and forth to filter the status of each task, clicking and taking to another window. I feel like if there are shortcuts that allow the user to change the status of an open item directly from the tasks, it would be easier for the reviewers. Also, I've noticed some of our clients have issues reviewing, they need to create a login and some just don't have the time, not sure if this is a bug but it could be resolved if its not supposed to happen.

author: Ahmad S.
pros: I particularly enjoyed ease of use for version control
cons: It keeps evolving to improve customer experience, so not much to dislike

review_title: Must have for any team working on a website
author: Nathan W.
pros: Zipboard has been amazing for quickly being able to point out complex or difficult to explain changes that need to be made on our website. Rather than write up a small book I can quickly use the provided tools to visually capture the problem and point, encircle, and write a note connected to the needed changes. It has saved us so much time and increased productivity. Highly recommend.
cons: Getting around the software can be a bit confusing at times. Once you get the hang of it, how things are organized, it starts to make more sense.

description: Good. Had one instance where it was slightly troubling trying to use but i don't think it was the software, i think it was something to do with the page that prevented zipboard from working and i haven't asked for help about it yet as no time.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: simple/easy to use, takes 5min to explain to someone how to use it if they're reviewing content.
cons: I don't like that it requires a browser extension, this can limit where this tool can be used greatly, which means I personally can't use it with every colleague as depends on where they are situated. Of course, I know its very difficult, possibly not doable currently to have software like this without requiring a browser extension. Also the logo...the 'zB' logo looks terrible and makes the software appear like it might be less professional/trustworthy than it is.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

author: Rob F.
pros: Easy to use. Like that fixes and changes are reported in context, you don't have to find the slide that has the error. Good for remote review. I like the link for a reviewer has a how-to video before they use the software. Yet some still don't follow it!
cons: I've had some SME reviewers mix up slide titles with comments on the content. In other words, they did add a comment on screen, they clicked the title and made the comment there. So it can be confusing for some.

author: Kristin S.
pros: Ability to quickly collaborate on projects with disparate team
cons: When inviting users, you must manually enter email addresses. (No smart complete/autofill by connecting your email or uploading list of company emails or something to that effect)

description: zipboard is easy to use and understand . having good UX and bug tracking functionality is amazing.
author: Hemant J.
pros: Bug Tracking I like the most in the software because of add watchers.
cons: Track Progress needs some more insights
author_duration: Used the software for: I used a free trial

review_title: Helps keep track of all the feedback
author: Aravindh B.
pros: 1. Easy to use 2. Easy to collaborate 3. Visibility
cons: 1. Another tool in the toolbox 2. Plugins for design tools
author_duration: Used the software for: I used a free trial

author: Tiffany L.
pros: Love that you can take screenshots of the page and show exactly what needs to be changed. I also like that you can assign tasks to specific individuals and set the priority of them so that everyone can manage their time and efforts better.
cons: The only hiccup we had was having developers use the site - it would have been better if double clicking a task on the kanban board would bring them to the screenshot directly and show the rest of the tasks that are also on that page
author_duration: Used the software for: I used a free trial

description: We are developing a new software system and Zipboard has allowed us to effectively work with a software developer thousands of kilometers away for the duration of the project without any face to face meetings.
author: Tobias S.
pros: Can add to tasks to screenshots and then review and modify in task list format
cons: Would be nice to be able to move review items around the screen capture once written. The monthly subscription is a little expensive.
author_profession: Senior Research Development and Projects Officer

author: Megan W.
pros: zipBoard enables you to comment on aspects of a PowerPoint or video and include the exact screenshot of what you're talking about. It makes understanding comments a lot easier and takes out the guessing. It's also great if you want a group of people to review your product, because it consolidates all of the reviews in one place.
cons: zipBoard can be confusing to use at first, and sometimes it doesn't work on certain internet browsers or computers, which is incredibly frustrating.

review_title: Great user experience, ease of use
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Easy to use, friendly and intuitive. Relevant information, organization. Helped our teams collaborate and share information with efficiency.
cons: None to note. The experience is pretty well thought out and includes most features that I needed.
author_duration: Used the software for: I used a free trial

review_title: worked well for what we needed
description: We used this in a one off to review a website between internal and external groups and it went very well. The best part was the organization and it was very intuitive.
author: Allison T.
pros: The tool was really easy to use and to navigate.
cons: I feel like I got way too many emails but wasn't clear on how to turn off notifications.
author_duration: Used the software for: I used a free trial

description: I use zipBoard to communicate changes and updates with clients; I work as a freelance web designer. zipBoard lets the clients tell me what modifications they would like to see with comments and tags. I like that it's lightweight too and I don't have to create extra storage on my end.
author: Ryan H.
